channel.title: Internet Society Opportunities
channel.description: Latest opportunities to get involved with Internet Society initiatives

item.title: Open Door Call "A Safer Internet Does Not Weaken the Safety Tools People Rely On – VPN Restrictions" (07:00 UTC)
item.description: Join an Open-Door Call to discuss the draft policy brief “A Safer Internet Does Not Weaken the Safety Tools People Rely On – VPN Restrictions.” These interactive sessions provide an opportunity to share feedback, ask questions, and contribute expertise to help shape the final recommendations.

item.title: APAC Regional Call
item.description: This edition of the APAC Regional Call will focus on Policy and Advocacy engagements, trends and priorities in the Asia Pacific region.

item.title: Community Consultation - VPN Restrictions
item.description: This draft policy brief explores the growing efforts by some governments to ban, restrict, or control the use of Virtual Private Networks (VPNs), and provides principles and recommendations for addressing related policy concerns.

item.title: Common Good Cyber Fund Grant Program
item.description: Launched in 2025, the CGCF is a pooled fund — backed by a mix of governments and philanthropic donors — designed to sustain nonprofits strengthening the public-interest cybersecurity ecosystem.

item.title: Global Encryption Day Events Funding
item.description: An Internet Society Foundation funding program that supports community-led events celebrating and defending strong end-to-end encryption, held annually around Global Encryption Day on 21 October.

item.title: Chapters Capacity Building Program 2026
item.description: The Chapters Capacity Building Program 2026 is a training series for Internet Society chapter leaders, delivered by Nonprofit Builder, offered at no cost for Internet Society chapters, online across four tracks — Impact Measurement, Conflict Resolution, Leadership & Governance, and Financial Management — as weekly 2-hour workshops from June to October 2026, in English, French, and Spanish.

item.title: Moderated Courses- Learning @Internet Society
item.description: Moderated courses are offered through the Learning @ Internet Society online platform, supported by a moderator and a tutor. The moderator ensures all administrative issues are addressed, and the tutor provides the technical expertise and answers all questions related to the subject matter.

item.title: Regional Working Groups
item.description: Our Regional Working Groups are community‑led spaces, facilitated by a community expert. Internet Society members and staff discuss and collaborate on Internet policy and advocacy connected to four key themes: Safer Internet, Connectivity, Open Internet, Internet Governance. These groups enable you to get directly involved with the issues that matter most to you. You can also connect with peers across your region, exchange knowledge, and contribute to practical outputs such as policy briefs, consultations, research, and community events.

item.title: Learning@ Internet Society
item.description: Our courses provide you with practical technical skills designed and delivered by Internet experts. You will gain knowledge and skills on how the Internet works, how it is governed, and how to keep the Internet and its users safe and secure.

item.title: ISOC Chapter Management Essentials
item.description: This course helps you learn what it takes to successfully run an Internet Society chapter.

item.title: Grant Application and Project Implementation Guidance
item.description: This course is designed for those interested in applying for grants through the Internet Society Foundation.

item.title: Internet Governance Forum Events Funding
item.description: The Internet Society Foundation’s Internet Governance Forum (IGF) funding program supports the organization of United Nations (UN) IGF Secretariat-recognized National and Regional Initiatives (NRIs) and National and Regional Schools on Internet Governance. The purpose of the IGF is to encourage open, non-commercial, and inclusive multi-stakeholder dialogue on issues related to Internet governance. The goal of Schools on Internet Governance is to ensure that diverse actors have the skills to participate in Internet governance structures and shape the future of the Internet.

item.title: Beyond the Net Grant Program
item.description: The Internet Society Foundation’s Beyond the Net Grant Program offers funding to support Internet Society Chapters to implement innovative projects that make a local impact by providing meaningful access to an open, trusted, and globally-connected Internet, as well as strengthening the Chapters’ presence in the surrounding community.

item.title: Join the Global Encryption Coalition
item.description:
Some governments and organizations are pushing to weaken encryption, which would create a dangerous precedent that compromises the security of billions of people worldwide. Actions in one country that undermine encryption threaten all of us.
With over 400 members distributed across 103 countries, the Global Encryption Coalition promotes and defends encryption in key countries and multilateral fora where it is under threat. It also supports efforts by companies to offer encrypted services to their users.
